cue_case: blocked company claim or explicit exclusion
reason: Blocked NVIDIA/GTC claims and the energy-per-token exclusion are guardrails, not ordinary citations; readers and editors must see them where the tempting claim appears.
allowed_final_layout: Inline row ID in prose; may also duplicate in sidenote if final layout needs it.
prohibited_move: Do not move CH16Q-017 or CH16Q-018 into a paragraph-level source note that a reader can miss.
next_action: Keep inline until a final note prototype proves the blocker remains visible at sentence level.

reason: A single measured estimate can carry one compact cue without hurting rhythm, and the row ID separates measured evidence from scenario language nearby.
allowed_final_layout: Inline row ID after the sentence or clause containing the estimate.
prohibited_move: Do not collapse measured and forecast rows into one unlabeled citation cluster.
next_action: Retain inline style unless final layout uses a same-line sidenote anchor.

reason: Forecast rows need visible scenario labeling because they are the easiest numbers to misread as happened history.
allowed_final_layout: Inline row ID paired with forecast wording; sidenote may repeat the scenario caveat.
prohibited_move: Do not move scenarios to a generic paragraph note or remove "projected," "modeled," or "scenario" wording.
next_action: Keep inline and test a sidenote only if the note label says scenario/forecast.

cue_case: two row IDs supporting one sentence from the same source family
reason: When one sentence contains two linked quantities from the same source family, a compact cluster is readable and preserves the shared caveat.
allowed_final_layout: One inline bracket with source IDs plus both CH16Q IDs.
prohibited_move: Do not split into two notes unless the sentence itself splits into two claims.
next_action: Keep current clusters unless prose is rewritten.

cue_case: paragraph with four exact rows across two sentence pairs
reason: The U.S. LBNL/DOE paragraph is accurate but visually dense; a sidenote could preserve row mapping while reducing inline clutter.
allowed_final_layout: Final layout may move the four row IDs to a numbered sidenote only if the sidenote maps TWh measured, TWh scenario, percent measured, and percent scenario separately.
prohibited_move: Do not collapse all four into "LBNL/DOE data" without distinguishing measured from scenario rows.
next_action: Use this paragraph as the test case for I-0058 final-note prototype.

reason: Operator-survey rows are weaker than primary reports and must remain visibly labeled as signals rather than universal engineering law.
allowed_final_layout: Inline cluster is acceptable; final footnote must say operator-survey signal if moved.
prohibited_move: Do not turn survey evidence into general facility law or hide the row IDs in a broad source note.
next_action: Keep inline until a final note can preserve the signal label.

cue_case: source-only bracket with no CH16Q row
current_examples: Opening source-only bracket before I-0053 cleanup; closing paragraph source-only brackets
reason: Source-only clutter can be combined because no row-level permission depends on the split.
allowed_final_layout: Combine adjacent source-only brackets or move to a paragraph source note.
prohibited_move: Do not collapse a bracket that contains a CH16Q ID under this rule.
next_action: Continue using I-0053 style for source-only cleanup.

current_examples: Any new TWh, MW, percent, PUE, rack-density, delay-risk, Wh/token, NVIDIA/GTC, or forecast sentence
reason: New exact quantities need CH16Q-style row permission before they enter prose.
allowed_final_layout: New row in data/chapter16_quant_claim_audit_i0043.tsv or successor table, then inline cue under the relevant rule.
prohibited_move: Do not add a new exact number and rely only on a source ID.
next_action: Create a successor CH16Q row before expanding Chapter 16 quantitative prose.
